articles.title: WHAT IF ADDING AUTH TO YOUR APP TOOK ONE COMMAND?
articles.summary: npx workos launches an AI agent [4], powered by Claude, that reads your project, detects your framework, and writes a complete auth integration directly into your existing codebase. It's not a template generator. It reads your code, understands your stack, and writes an integration that fits.

articles.title: See how it works →
ANTHROPIC REPORTED LARGE-SCALE DISTILLATION ATTEMPTS
articles.summary: Anthropic accused DeepSeek, Moonshot AI, and MiniMax of creating over 24,000 fake accounts to generate roughly 16 million Claude interactions aimed at replicating its agentic reasoning, tool use, and coding capabilities.

articles.title: OPENAI PREPARES NEW CHATGPT PRO LITE TIER PRICED AT $100 MONTHLY
articles.summary: OpenAI plans to offer a new subscription tier called ChatGPT Pro Lite. At $100 per month, the new plan could serve users who hit Plus rate limits regularly but do not need the unlimited access that Pro delivers. It could be a way to accommodate Codex usage as well. No release date has been confirmed yet, and the exact feature set remains unclear.

articles.title: PERPLEXITY TESTS MESSAGES INTEGRATION AND USAGE CREDITS
articles.summary: Perplexity is testing a Messages connector for its Comet browser, enhancing local integration with Apple's Messages app. This appeals to macOS power users by merging communication history with web data. A new "Usage and Credits" feature allows users to buy additional credits, addressing backlash from reduced Pro plan limits.

articles.title: GPT-5 CODEX RAN A 25-HOUR CODING SPRINT
articles.summary: OpenAI described a long-horizon stress test where GPT-5.3-Codex, given a blank repository and full access, built a design tool over ~25 hours using ~13M tokens and producing ~30k lines of code.

articles.title: SWE-BENCH VERIFIED STOPPED BEING A FRONTIER CODING METRIC
articles.summary: SWE-bench Verified no longer reliably measures frontier coding progress because many tasks have flawed tests that rejected correct fixes and because benchmark contamination has made training exposure a significant factor in scores.

articles.title: MATHEMATICS IN THE LIBRARY OF BABEL
articles.summary: Daniel Litt argues that current limitations are real but likely temporary, not fundamental blockers. Litt now expects AI to autonomously resolve mildly interesting open conjectures as early as late 2026 and suggests AI's long-term impact on mathematics may exceed that of the computer. Even in a future where machines can generate vast bodies of correct math, he argues human mathematicians will still matter because the core motivation remains understanding, not just producing results.

articles.title: GIVE ANY AI AGENT ACCESS TO GOOGLE SEARCH WITH SERPAPI
credits/month
AWS LAUNCHES STRANDS LABS TO GIVE DEVELOPERS A SANDBOX FOR AUTONOMOUS AI
articles.summary: AWS Strands Labs is a new GitHub organization aimed at helping developers explore and experiment with cutting-edge AI techniques. It is currently available for three specific projects: robots, robots sim, and AI functions. AWS considers these areas the ones that most clearly reveal what changes when software becomes agentic. Strands Labs gives AWS and the broader community a dedicated space to experiment boldly.

articles.title: THE FIRST FULLY GENERAL COMPUTER ACTION MODEL
articles.summary: FDM-1, a foundation model for computer use, efficiently trains on 11 million hours of video to autonomously handle tasks like CAD, driving, and fuzzing. Unlike traditional models that rely on expensive, limited annotation, FDM-1 uses a video encoder that compresses 2 hours of video into 1M tokens and predicts actions with inverse dynamics for scalable training. With enhanced context processing, FDM-1 showcases significant advancements in executing long-horizon tasks and exploring complex state trees in GUI environments.

articles.title: SELF-AWARE REASONING EFFICIENCY
articles.summary: SAGE introduces a self-aware sampling approach that enables large reasoning models to decide when to stop thinking, reducing unnecessary computation. Its companion method, SAGE-RL, distills these efficient reasoning patterns into standard pass@1 inference, reporting accuracy improvement with fewer tokens across six challenging math benchmarks.

articles.title: MACHINEAUTH
articles.summary: MachineAuth provides secure OAuth 2.0 client credentials flow for AI agents, enabling machine-to-machine authentication. Key features include JWT token generation, agent management, and scope-based permissions, all with simple JSON file storage and no databases required.

articles.title: TAALAS HC1: ABSURDLY FAST, PER-USER INFERENCE AT 17,000 TOKENS/SECOND
articles.summary: Taalas has been quietly building a model-on-silicon chip that bakes an LLM directly into hardware to deliver absurdly fast, per-user inference. The HC1 chip runs Meta's Llama 3.1 8B at extreme speed by effectively hardwiring the model into silicon. It delivers inference at around 17,000 tokens per second per user. This is just the first version - the next iterations are already geared toward much better fidelity.

articles.title: OPENAI LANDS MULTIYEAR DEALS WITH CONSULTING GIANTS IN ENTERPRISE PUSH
articles.summary: OpenAI is entering into multiyear partnerships with consulting firms Accenture, Boston Consulting Group, Capgemini, and McKinsey & Co. to help deploy its enterprise platform, Frontier. Frontier is an intelligence layer that stitches together disparate systems and data within an organization to make it easier for companies to manage, deploy, and build AI agents. OpenAI decided to partner with the consulting firms because they have existing relationships with enterprises and deep knowledge about how those businesses operate. They will help OpenAI roll out its technology at scale.

articles.title: FULLY REMOTE)
articles.summary: As the first engineer on TLDR's new Applied AI team, you'll build AI agents and composable Claude Skills to let non-technical teammates create their own AI workflows. Learn more [21].

articles.title: IBM STOCK DIVES AFTER ANTHROPIC POINTS OUT AI CAN REWRITE COBOL FAST
articles.summary: Anthropic claims that Claude Code tools can accelerate the refactoring of apps written in COBOL.

articles.title: DEEPSEEK V4: RUMORS VS REALITY FOR THE NEXT BIG CODING MODEL
articles.summary: DeepSeek V4, a highly anticipated open source model with a 1M+ context based on Engram architecture, will likely be released before the end of the month.

articles.title: ASML ANNOUNCES BREAKTHROUGH IN EUV LIGHT SOURCE TO BOOST CHIP OUTPUT
articles.summary: The development could enable chipmakers to produce up to 50% more semiconductors by the end of the decade.
